samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>
        Implement an asynchronous FIFO with configurable parameters DEPTH and WIDTH.
        The FIFO consists of several parts:
        1. Dual-port RAM for data storage
        2. Data write controller
        3. Data read controller
        4. Read pointer synchronizer
        5. Write pointer synchronizer
        Empty and Full conditions are determined using Gray codes for read/write pointers.
    </description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal. Active low reset.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal. Active low reset.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal. Triggers write operations.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal. Triggers read operations.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal. Indicates if FIFO is full.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal. Indicates if FIFO is empty.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output.</output>
    </ports>
    
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="DEPTH">Number of memory locations in the FIFO.</constant>
        <constant id="WIDTH">Data width of each memory location.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        // Registers for storing binary addresses
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr;
        
        // Registers for storing Gray-encoded addresses
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] wptr;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] rptr;
        
        // Registers for buffering read and write pointers
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] wptr_buff;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] rptr_buff;
        
        // Register for storing synchronized read pointer
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] rptr_syn;
        
        // Sequential logic for updating binary and Gray-coded addresses
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if (!wrstn) begin
                waddr <= 0;
            end else if (winc && !wfull) begin
                waddr <= waddr + 1;
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if (!rrstn) begin
                raddr <= 0;
            end else if (rinc && !rempty) begin
                raddr <= raddr + 1;
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if (!wrstn) begin
                wptr <= 0;
            end else if (winc && !wfull) begin
                wptr <= {1'b0, waddr} ^ ({1'b0, waddr} >> 1);
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if (!rrstn) begin
                rptr <= 0;
            end else if (rinc && !rempty) begin
                rptr <= {1'b0, raddr} ^ ({1'b0, raddr} >> 1);
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if (!wrstn) begin
                wptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                wptr_buff <= wptr;
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if (!rrstn) begin
                rptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                rptr_buff <= rptr;
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if (!rrstn) begin
                rptr_syn <= 0;
            end else begin
                rptr_syn <= rptr_buff;
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>
        // Instantiate dual-port RAM
        dual_port_RAM #(DEPTH, WIDTH) ram_inst(
            .wclk(wclk),
            .wenc(!wfull & winc),
            .waddr(waddr),
            .wdata(wdata),
            
            .rclk(rclk),
            .renc(!rempty & rinc),
            .raddr(raddr),
            .rdata(rdata)
        );
        
        // Assign full and empty signals
        assign wfull = (wptr[$clog2(DEPTH)] != rptr_syn[$clog2(DEPTH)]) &&
                       (wptr[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] == rptr_syn[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]);
                       
        assign rempty = (wptr == rptr);
    </implementation>
endmodule
